Home / Services / Stump Grinding

Stump Grinding

Complete stump removal to eliminate hazards and improve your landscape.

After a tree is removed, the stump can be an unsightly and hazardous obstacle. Stump grinding is the most effective way to completely eliminate the stump, preventing regrowth, eliminating tripping hazards, and allowing you to reclaim your yard. Tree Service By Northwest uses specialized stump grinding equipment to grind the stump down below ground level, leaving you with a clean and usable space.

Seattle's damp climate makes stump removal particularly important. Stumps left in the ground can attract pests, promote fungal growth, and become a breeding ground for moss and mildew. This is especially problematic for homes in areas like Ballard and Fremont, where moisture is prevalent. Our stump grinding service prevents these issues and restores your property's beauty.

We handle stumps of all sizes and in all locations. Our team is skilled at navigating tight spaces and minimizing disruption to your existing landscaping. We also take care of the cleanup, removing the grindings and leaving your yard ready for planting or other uses.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Stump Assessment - Evaluating the size and location of the stump.
  2. 02 Step 2: Grinding - Using specialized equipment to grind the stump below ground level.
  3. 03 Step 3: Cleanup - Removing grindings and debris.
  4. 04 Step 4: Site Restoration - Filling the hole with topsoil and preparing it for planting.

Benefits

  • Eliminate tripping hazards
  • Prevent regrowth
  • Improve aesthetics
  • Prevent pest infestations

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does stump grinding take?

The time required depends on the size of the stump and its location. Most stump grinding jobs can be completed in a few hours.

Will stump grinding damage my yard?

We take precautions to minimize damage to your yard. We use protective mats and carefully maneuver our equipment to avoid disturbing surrounding vegetation.

What happens to the grindings?

We can either remove the grindings or use them to fill the hole, depending on your preference. Grindings can also be used as mulch.
